title: RNAS Fearn (HMS Owl)

infobox.value: [European theatre of World War II](/wiki/European_theatre_of_World_War_II "European theatre of World War II")

(**RNAS Fearn**; or **HMS *Owl***) is a former [Royal Navy](/wiki/Royal_Navy "Royal Navy") [Fleet Air Arm](/wiki/Fleet_Air_Arm "Fleet Air Arm") [airbase](/wiki/Airbase "Airbase"), located 5.4 miles (8.7 km) southeast of [Tain](/wiki/Tain "Tain"), [Scottish Highlands](/wiki/Scottish_Highlands "Scottish Highlands") and 21.9 miles (35.2 km) northeast of [Inverness](/wiki/Inverness "Inverness"), Scottish Highlands, Scotland. The Tower has now been converted to residential use. See *[Restoration Man](/wiki/Restoration_Man "Restoration Man")* George Clarke.

**Royal Naval Air Station Fearn** (**RNAS Fearn**; or **HMS *Owl***) is a former [Royal Navy](/wiki/Royal_Navy "Royal Navy") [Fleet Air Arm](/wiki/Fleet_Air_Arm "Fleet Air Arm") [airbase](/wiki/Airbase "Airbase"), located 5.4 miles (8.7 km) southeast of [Tain](/wiki/Tain "Tain"), [Scottish Highlands](/wiki/Scottish_Highlands "Scottish Highlands") and 21.9 miles (35.2 km) northeast of [Inverness](/wiki/Inverness "Inverness"), Scottish Highlands, Scotland. The Tower has now been converted to residential use. See *[Restoration Man](/wiki/Restoration_Man "Restoration Man")* George Clarke.

Situated around 1 mile (2 km) from the north west shore of the [Moray Firth](/wiki/Moray_Firth "Moray Firth"), the airfield is 8.5 miles (14 km) north east of the town and [port](/wiki/Port "Port") of [Invergordon](/wiki/Invergordon "Invergordon") and 1 mile (2 km) south east of the village of Fearn. Notable landmarks include [Tarbat Ness](/wiki/Tarbat_Ness "Tarbat Ness") and [Cromarty Firth](/wiki/Cromarty_Firth "Cromarty Firth").[[1]](#cite_note-RNRA-Fearn-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RNAS_Fearn&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Royal Navy acquired the airbase when on 15 July 1942 it was transferred from the RAF to the [Admiralty](/wiki/Admiralty_(United_Kingdom) "Admiralty (United Kingdom)") and was known as Royal Naval Air Station Fearn (RNAS Fearn). On 11 October it was commissioned as HMS *Owl*.[[1]](#cite_note-RNRA-Fearn-1) The airbase had initially opened in late 1941 as a satellite for [RAF Tain](/wiki/RAF_Tain "RAF Tain"), known as RAF Fearn, before the Fleet Air Arm took it over.[[2]](#cite_note-ABCT-2) HMS *Owl* was home to the Royal Navy’s Barracuda Operational Training Unit, No. 1 Barracuda Servicing Unit and No. 3 Barracuda Servicing Unit along with No. 1 Avenger Servicing Unit. There was also No. 2 Torpedo School. The site also included accommodation for disembarked squadrons.[[1]](#cite_note-RNRA-Fearn-1)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RNAS_Fearn&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Units")]

A number of units were here at some point:

* [No. 8 Advanced Flying Training School RAF](/wiki/No._8_Advanced_Flying_Training_School_RAF "No. 8 Advanced Flying Training School RAF")[[2]](#cite_note-ABCT-2)
* [708 Naval Air Squadron](/wiki/708_Naval_Air_Squadron "708 Naval Air Squadron")[[3]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTESturtivant,_Ballance199431-3)

* [825 Naval Air Squadron](/wiki/825_Naval_Air_Squadron "825 Naval Air Squadron")[[20]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTESturtivant,_Ballance1994219-20)

## Current use

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RNAS_Fearn&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Current use")]

The site is currently used as farmland.[[2]](#cite_note-ABCT-2) On the southeast edge, a new, separate, aerodrome has been created by the name of "Easter Airfield".[[27]](#cite_note-27) The control tower is now a private residence owned. It was in a 2015 episode of [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4")'s *[The Restoration Man](/wiki/The_Restoration_Man "The Restoration Man")*.[[28]](#cite_note-28) A lot of the original buildings on the other side of the camp are derelict and only have trash from fly-tipping, crows and bugs in them.
